hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic complementarity at position 5 and introducing hydrophobic residues at position 11 will improve SSTR2 selectivity by strengthening interactions with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 residues while avoiding off-target contacts.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is valid, but the structural quality of the candidates is a limiting factor. Tightening structural constraints and focusing mutations on non-pharmacophore positions will improve both structural quality and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reducing the clash tolerance will enforce better structural quality by eliminating candidates with steric clashes that may destabilize the protein or reduce binding specificity.

To improve selectivity, we must disrupt off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1, 2, 5, 6, 11, 12) while preserving the pharmacophore (positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14). Focusing mutations on these positions can help break interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5.
